free_text: Observation of large incomplete fusion in 16O + 103Rh
system at 3.5 MeV/nucleon
The targets of 103Rh of thickness ~ 2.0 mg/cm2 were
prepared from the spectroscopically pure foils of
natural Rhodium (103Rh) employing rolling technique.
The targets were fixed on Al-holders of 1.2 x 1.2 cm2
size and concentric hole of ~ 10 mm diam. Two stacks,
each containing three 103Rh-target foils were
irradiated by 16O7+ beam at energies . 80 and 85 MeV,
separately.
The thickness of each target was
determined by alpha-transmission method.
The detector used in this experiment was pre-
calibrated for energy and efficiency using various
standard gamma -sources, viz., 60Co, 133Ba and 152Eu
at different source-detector separations.
Total uncertainty includes:
The error in the thickness of the sample
The error in the efficiency determination
due to the statistical fluctuations in counts is
less than 2%
Dead time of the spectrometer was kept
less than 10% by suitably adjusting sample-detector
distance
* the error due to beam current and
* uncertainty in the determination of geometry
dependent spectrometer efficiency.
The overall errors including statistical errors are
estimated to be ~ 15%, excluding the uncertainty in
branching ratio, decay constant, etc., which have
been taken from the "Table of Radioactive Isotopes"
(E. Browne, R.B. Firestone, Table of Radioactive
Isotopes, Wiley, New York, 1986.)
Data were received from Dr. B.P.Singh
SD and Damewan Suchiang (dmwnshg@gmail.com),
Joseph Jeremiah joeatchen@gmail.com

from cumulative cross-sections using the successive
